title: viola canina subsp. pumila
intro: Viola canina subsp. pumila, also known as dwarf dog violet, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the violet family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, as it has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems and digestive issues, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Viola canina subsp. pumila include its ability to soothe coughs, reduce inflammation, and calm digestive issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to support respiratory and digestive health.
